examples.title: Basic Exception Handling
examples.description: Demonstrates basic try-catch-throw mechanism with division by zero handling
examples.explanation: The divide function throws runtime_error when divisor is zero. The catch blocks handle specific exceptions with catch by reference.

examples.title: Custom Exception Class
examples.description: Shows creation and use of custom exception class derived from std::exception
examples.explanation: MyException derives from std::exception, overrides what() method. Demonstrates custom exception throwing and catching.

examples.title: Exception in Constructor
examples.description: Demonstrates exception handling in constructors and why destructors should not throw
examples.explanation: Constructor throws when Resource creation fails. Destructor is NOT called for failed construction. Demonstrates proper exception handling in constructors.

examples.title: Stack Unwinding Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how stack unwinding works when exceptions propagate through multiple function calls
examples.explanation: Demonstrates stack unwinding: objects 1, 2, 3 are constructed but when exception is thrown, destructors are called in reverse order (3, 2, 1) as stack unwinds.
